recommendations.title: A City of Sadness
recommendations.overview: The story of a family embroiled in the "White Terror," the Kuomintang government's anti-communist political repression that was wrought on the Taiwanese people from 1947-1987.

recommendations.title: The Time to Live and the Time to Die
recommendations.overview: An autobiographical film based on Taiwanese director Hou Hsiao-hsien's memories of his youth growing up in Taiwan after emigrating from mainland China.

recommendations.title: Millennium Mambo
recommendations.overview: Vicky recalls her romances with her exes Hao Hao and Jack in the neon-lit clubs of Taipei.

recommendations.title: Dust in the Wind
recommendations.overview: A-yuan and A-yun are both from the small mining town of Jio-fen. They move to Taipei, where A-yuan is an apprentice by day and goes to night school, and A-yun works as a helper at a tailors. Everyone thinks they are meant for each other, and so do th

recommendations.title: Flowers of Shanghai
recommendations.overview: At the end of the 19th century, Shanghai is divided into several foreign concessions. In the British concession, a number of luxurious “flower houses” are reserved for the male elite of the city. Since Chinese dignitaries are not allowed to frequent

recommendations.title: The Assassin
recommendations.overview: 9th century China. Ten year old general’s daughter Nie Yinniang is abducted by a nun who initiates her into the martial arts, transforming her into an exceptional assassin charged with eliminating cruel and corrupt local governors. One day, having fa

recommendations.title: Life in a Year
recommendations.overview: A 17 year old finds out that his girlfriend is dying, so he sets out to give her an entire life, in the last year she has left.

recommendations.title: More the Merrier
recommendations.overview: Multiple stories about the oscillating world of couple relationships and how difficult it can be to separate sex from love.

recommendations.title: To Each His Own Cinema
recommendations.overview: Commissioned to mark the 60th anniversary of the Cannes Film Festival, "To Each His Own Cinema" brought together 33 of the world's pre-eminent filmmakers to produce short pieces exploring the multifarious facets of cinema and their perspective on the
recommendations.director: Raymond Depardon, Andrei Konchalovsky, Lars von Trier, Chen Kaige, Michael Cimino, Atom Egoyan, Wim Wenders, Manoel de Oliveira, Hou Hsiao-hsien, Jean-Pierre Dardenne, Jane Campion, Zhang Yimou, Ken Loach, Nanni Moretti, Elia Suleiman, Alejandro G. recommendations.title: Love Actually
recommendations.overview: Eight very different couples deal with their love lives in various loosely interrelated tales all set during a frantic month before Christmas in London.
